attestation.statement: Clinical scope attested as it presently stands.
attestation.note: OFF_FUTURE areas require the Scope Activation Gate (jurisdictional authority + pharmacist training + regulatory confirmation + MIRAGE corpus attestation) before moving to ACTIVE. Activation and service_type are per-condition x per-jurisdiction.

activation_states.ACTIVE: Live in Phase-0 pilot within current pharmacist scope.
activation_states.OFF_FUTURE: Held ready in the taxonomy; not active until the Scope Activation Gate passes.
activation_states.EXCLUDE_ONLY: Never a managed primary; recognition-and-escalation only.

jurisdiction_note: QLD Extended Practice Authority live (from 1 Jul 2025); WA Enhanced Access Community Pharmacy Pilot service delivery ~2027. Per-condition state authority to be confirmed at activation.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Unstable vitals (>1 deranged; high-grade fever, hypotension, tachycardia, tachypnoea)
Poor perfusion (pale, cool, clammy, mottled, cold extremities)
condition_specific.refer_if: Stable vitals (only low-grade fever), intact mental status, localised flank/CVA pain, preserved urine output, warm well-perfused skin
condition_specific.note: Stable localised -> REFER; systemic instability/sepsis -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Bilateral obstruction or reduced/absent urine output (obstructive AKI)
Intractable pain or vomiting preventing oral intake
condition_specific.refer_if: Classic unilateral loin-to-groin colic, afebrile, passing urine, pain controllable, two functioning kidneys
condition_specific.note: Uncomplicated colic -> REFER; obstruction + infection, solitary kidney, or anuria -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Clot retention or acute urinary retention
Heavy visible haematuria with haemodynamic concern or symptomatic anaemia
condition_specific.refer_if: Painless visible or microscopic haematuria, no retention or anaemia (urgent GP/urology referral, not ED)
condition_specific.note: Painless haematuria = urgent referral; clot retention or symptomatic bleeding -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Vertigo or new sensorineural hearing loss
Severe headache, meningism or other intracranial features
condition_specific.refer_if: Chronic foul-smelling ear discharge with conductive hearing loss, no neurological features
condition_specific.note: Chronic discharge/hearing loss -> REFER; facial palsy, vertigo or intracranial signs -> IMMEDIATE.

condition_specific.refer_if: Isolated lower-motor-neuron palsy with forehead involvement (e.g. Bell's palsy), no other deficit
condition_specific.note: Forehead sparing = UMN -> stroke -> IMMEDIATE; isolated LMN -> REFER.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

condition_specific.refer_if: Isolated, non-acute, no other deficit
condition_specific.note: Acute / multi-nerve / with red flags -> IMMEDIATE; isolated non-acute -> REFER.

exclusions.condition: Necrotising otitis externa with intracranial extension
exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Clinical diagnosis of compartment syndrome established
Pain severe / increasing / out of proportion
condition_specific.refer_if: Diagnosis in doubt / clinically unsure / underwhelming presentation
condition_specific.note: True compartment syndrome is a clinical diagnosis (5 P's) -> IMMEDIATE; if unsure/underwhelming -> REFER.

exclusions.action: Mandatory child-protection / safeguarding escalation regardless of clinical acuity; no reassuring-refer branch.
exclusions.note: Safeguarding pathway (R19) — not acuity-based.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Neurovascular compromise distal to wound (pallor, pulselessness, paraesthesia, cold, prolonged capillary refill)
Loss of active movement of a digit/joint (suspected tendon rupture)
Sensory loss in a nerve distribution distal to the wound
Deep laceration or high-pressure injection over a tendon, joint or neurovascular bundle
condition_specific.refer_if: Superficial wound with intact distal sensation, full active movement and normal perfusion
condition_specific.note: Any distal neurovascular or tendon deficit -> IMMEDIATE; superficial, fully intact -> REFER.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Rapidly progressive, ulcerating or new tumour-stage nodules
Lymphadenopathy or B symptoms (fever, night sweats, weight loss)
condition_specific.refer_if: Chronic fixed patches/plaques, no systemic features or nodal disease
condition_specific.note: Indolent patch/plaque disease -> REFER; erythroderma, tumours or systemic features -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Secondary bacterial infection with systemic features
condition_specific.refer_if: Limited crusting in an otherwise well, immunocompetent host
condition_specific.note: Highly contagious; widespread crusting, immunocompromise or secondary sepsis -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Acute hot, swollen joint where septic arthritis cannot be excluded
Rapidly progressive or deforming joint destruction
Spinal involvement with new neurological signs
condition_specific.refer_if: Chronic joint pain/stiffness with known psoriasis, no acute hot joint
condition_specific.note: Chronic inflammatory arthritis -> REFER; acute hot joint (?septic) or neuro signs -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Rapidly enlarging or ulcerating skin nodules
Lymphadenopathy or B symptoms (fever, night sweats, weight loss)
condition_specific.refer_if: Indolent, stable plaques or nodules, no systemic features
condition_specific.note: Stable indolent lesions -> REFER; rapid growth, ulceration or systemic features -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Fever and malaise with sudden severe ulcerating/haemorrhagic nodulocystic acne
Systemic upset (the defining feature vs severe acne)
condition_specific.refer_if: Severe nodulocystic acne without systemic symptoms
condition_specific.note: Acne fulminans is systemic by definition; any systemic features -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Renal involvement (new oedema, hypertension, reduced urine output)
Neuropsychiatric features (seizure, psychosis, acute focal deficit)
Serositis with pleuritic chest pain or breathlessness
Severe cytopenia (unexplained bleeding, bruising or recurrent infection)
condition_specific.refer_if: Mild mucocutaneous or joint symptoms (malar rash, arthralgia), no organ involvement
condition_specific.note: Mild mucocutaneous/joint disease -> REFER; any organ-threatening feature -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Spreading cellulitis or deep abscess formation
condition_specific.refer_if: Superficial pustular folliculitis (often after prolonged acne antibiotics), systemically well
condition_specific.note: Superficial pustules -> REFER; deep/spreading infection or immunocompromise -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Unilateral nasal obstruction with blood-stained discharge or recurrent epistaxis
Facial numbness, facial swelling or cranial neuropathy
condition_specific.refer_if: Bilateral symptoms typical of benign rhinosinusitis, no unilateral red flags
condition_specific.note: Bilateral benign picture -> REFER; unilateral bleeding, orbital or cranial-nerve signs -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Saddle-nose deformity or nasal crusting with systemic illness
condition_specific.refer_if: Isolated chronic rhinosinusitis or nasal crusting without systemic features
condition_specific.note: Isolated ENT picture -> REFER; pulmonary-renal or eye/nerve involvement -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Clear unilateral watery nasal discharge after head trauma or sinus/skull-base surgery
Salty/sweet taste, positional discharge or halo/target sign
condition_specific.refer_if: Clear rhinorrhoea readily explained by allergic/vasomotor rhinitis, no trauma or meningism
condition_specific.note: Suspected true CSF leak carries meningitis risk -> IMMEDIATE; typical rhinitis -> REFER.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Unintentional weight loss or palpable epigastric mass
Persistent vomiting or iron-deficiency anaemia features
condition_specific.refer_if: Mild dyspepsia in a lower-risk patient, no alarm features
condition_specific.note: Dyspepsia without alarm features -> REFER; any ALARM feature -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

condition_specific.refer_if: Intermittent reflux symptoms without dysphagia or weight loss
condition_specific.note: Uncomplicated reflux -> REFER; progressive dysphagia, bleeding or bolus obstruction -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: New/progressive severe spinal pain, especially thoracic or night pain
Neurological deficit (leg weakness, sensory level, gait disturbance)
Bladder/bowel dysfunction or saddle anaesthesia (cord/cauda compression)
Known cancer history with new back pain
condition_specific.refer_if: Mechanical back pain, no neurological deficit, no cancer history or red flags
condition_specific.note: Mechanical pain -> REFER; cord/cauda red flags or cancer history -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Fever with severe unremitting or night spinal pain
Risk factors (IV drug use, recent bacteraemia, immunosuppression)
Focal spinal tenderness with systemic upset
condition_specific.refer_if: Mechanical back pain, afebrile, no risk factors or neurological signs
condition_specific.note: Mechanical pain -> REFER; fever + spinal pain, risk factors or neuro signs -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Bone pain with suspected pathological fracture
Anaemia or recurrent infections; cord-compression signs
condition_specific.refer_if: Nonspecific fatigue or bone aches without CRAB features
condition_specific.note: Nonspecific -> REFER; any CRAB feature or cord compression -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Haemodynamically significant bleeding or symptomatic anaemia
Postmenopausal bleeding with associated pelvic mass or pain
condition_specific.refer_if: Postmenopausal or abnormal uterine bleeding, haemodynamically stable (urgent gynaecology referral, not ED)
condition_specific.note: Any postmenopausal bleeding = urgent referral; heavy bleeding/anaemia -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Acute severe abdominal/pelvic pain (?torsion or rupture)
Abdominal distension with ascites and breathlessness
condition_specific.refer_if: Persistent bloating, early satiety or pelvic discomfort without acute features
condition_specific.note: Persistent vague symptoms -> REFER; acute abdomen, obstruction or tense ascites -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.
Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Neurological features (severe headache, neck stiffness, confusion - ?meningoencephalitis)
Oral/oesophageal candidiasis or other opportunistic infection (advanced immunosuppression)
Severe or rapidly worsening systemic illness
condition_specific.refer_if: Self-limiting glandular-fever-type illness with a risk exposure, systemically well
condition_specific.note: Mild seroconversion picture -> REFER (and consider PEP window); neuro or opportunistic features -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Rapid onset with weight loss/pigmentation suggesting ectopic ACTH/malignancy
condition_specific.refer_if: Gradual classic features (central obesity, striae, moon face), systemically stable
condition_specific.note: Classic gradual picture -> REFER; severe metabolic derangement or ectopic-ACTH features -> IMMEDIATE.

condition_specific.refer_if: Typical fatigue, cold intolerance, weight gain and constipation, systemically stable
condition_specific.note: Stable hypothyroid symptoms -> REFER; myxoedema-coma features -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Features suggesting acute PE or pulmonary hypertension
condition_specific.refer_if: Chronic stable peripheral oedema/raised JVP without acute decompensation
condition_specific.note: Chronic compensated -> REFER; acute decompensation or suspected PE -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Visual loss or rapidly progressive visual-field change
Sixth-nerve palsy/diplopia or transient visual obscurations
condition_specific.refer_if: Chronic headache without visual symptoms or signs
condition_specific.note: Headache alone -> REFER; any visual loss/papilloedema -> IMMEDIATE (sight-threatening).

escalate_to_immediate_if: Non-healing oral ulcer or lesion persisting >3 weeks
condition_specific.refer_if: Painful ulcer with an obvious cause that is resolving within a few weeks
condition_specific.note: Resolving explained ulcer -> REFER; persistent/indurated lesion or nodal disease -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Spreading infection (facial swelling, trismus, dysphagia/drooling - ?Ludwig's angina/noma)
condition_specific.refer_if: Painful, bleeding, ulcerated interdental papillae with halitosis, systemically well
condition_specific.note: Localised NUG -> REFER; spreading deep-space infection or immunocompromise -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Mucosal bleeding, easy bruising or petechiae
condition_specific.refer_if: Gingival swelling without systemic features (still urgent haematology referral)
condition_specific.note: Suspected leukaemia is urgent; any cytopenia feature (bleeding, fever, pallor) -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: GI bleeding or perforation (severe abdominal pain, peritonism)
Sustained high fever with systemic toxicity/relative bradycardia
condition_specific.refer_if: Returned traveller with sustained fever, systemically stable, no peritonism
condition_specific.note: Stable febrile traveller -> REFER (notifiable); perforation, bleeding or toxicity -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Warning signs: abdominal pain/tenderness or persistent vomiting
condition_specific.refer_if: Febrile returned traveller, no warning signs, tolerating oral fluids
condition_specific.note: Uncomplicated dengue -> REFER (notifiable); any warning sign -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: SVC obstruction (facial/neck swelling, distended neck veins)
Chest/bone pain, hoarseness or new focal neurology (?brain metastasis)
condition_specific.refer_if: Chronic cough in a smoker without haemoptysis, weight loss or other red flags
condition_specific.note: Chronic cough alone -> REFER; haemoptysis, weight loss or SVCO -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Severe breathlessness at rest or unable to speak in full sentences
condition_specific.refer_if: Mild increase in breathlessness/sputum, still functional, no hypoxia or confusion
condition_specific.note: Mild exacerbation -> REFER; hypoxia, exhaustion or altered consciousness -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Headache with vomiting or other cranial-nerve signs (brainstem compression)
condition_specific.refer_if: Unilateral sensorineural hearing loss/tinnitus with mild imbalance, no other cranial-nerve signs
condition_specific.note: Isolated audiovestibular symptoms -> REFER; facial/other cranial-nerve or brainstem signs -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Vertigo or new sensorineural hearing loss
Severe headache, meningism or other intracranial features
condition_specific.refer_if: Chronic foul-smelling ear discharge with conductive hearing loss, no neurological features
condition_specific.note: Chronic discharge/hearing loss -> REFER; facial palsy, vertigo or intracranial signs -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Unilateral hearing loss or serous otitis media
condition_specific.refer_if: Bilateral nasal symptoms typical of benign rhinitis, no neck mass or cranial-nerve signs
condition_specific.note: Benign bilateral picture -> REFER; unilateral neck mass, ear or cranial-nerve signs -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Unable to maintain oral intake / hypoxia
condition_specific.refer_if: Mild community-acquired pneumonia, systemically well, normal mentation, tolerating oral intake
condition_specific.note: Low-severity CAP -> REFER; CRB-65 features (confusion, RR>=30, low BP) -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Severe pleuritic chest pain with breathlessness
condition_specific.refer_if: Resolving pleuritic symptoms after pneumonia, systemically well (low suspicion of collection)
condition_specific.note: Suspected empyema is a drainable sepsis source; ongoing fever, hypoxia or toxicity -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Fever with a new or changing heart murmur
Embolic phenomena (splinter haemorrhages, Janeway lesions, Osler nodes, focal neuro deficit, limb ischaemia)
IV drug use or prosthetic valve with fever
condition_specific.refer_if: Nonspecific low-grade illness where IE is a differential but suspicion is low and patient is stable
condition_specific.note: Low-suspicion, stable -> REFER; fever + murmur/embolic signs or prosthetic-valve/IVDU fever -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Tense ascites with breathlessness or fever (?spontaneous bacterial peritonitis)
Deepening jaundice with reduced urine output (?hepatorenal)
condition_specific.refer_if: Compensated cirrhosis / stigmata of chronic liver disease, systemically well
condition_specific.note: Compensated -> REFER; any feature of decompensation -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Severe RUQ pain or acute abdomen with hypotension (?tumour rupture)
condition_specific.refer_if: Lesion suspected on surveillance imaging, stable, no decompensation
condition_specific.note: Stable surveillance finding -> REFER; rupture, decompensation or rapid decline -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Marked malaise with rapidly worsening jaundice (?acute liver failure)
condition_specific.refer_if: Chronic fatigue or mildly deranged LFTs, systemically stable
condition_specific.note: Chronic/mild -> REFER; features of acute liver failure -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Tense ascites with breathlessness or fever (?spontaneous bacterial peritonitis)
Deepening jaundice with reduced urine output (?hepatorenal)
condition_specific.refer_if: Compensated cirrhosis / stigmata of chronic liver disease, systemically well
condition_specific.note: Compensated -> REFER; any feature of decompensation -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Severe RUQ pain or acute abdomen with hypotension (?tumour rupture)
condition_specific.refer_if: Lesion suspected on surveillance imaging, stable, no decompensation
condition_specific.note: Stable surveillance finding -> REFER; rupture, decompensation or rapid decline -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Family history of sudden cardiac death
condition_specific.refer_if: Incidental murmur or family history, asymptomatic, no red-flag symptoms
condition_specific.note: Asymptomatic -> REFER; exertional syncope, chest pain or arrhythmia -> IMMEDIATE (SCD risk).

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Severe breathlessness at rest or hypoxia
Signs of right-heart-failure decompensation (worsening oedema/ascites with hypotension)
condition_specific.refer_if: Exertional breathlessness, stable, no syncope or right heart failure
condition_specific.note: Stable exertional dyspnoea -> REFER; syncope, chest pain or decompensation -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: SVC obstruction (facial/neck swelling, distended neck veins)
Chest/bone pain, hoarseness or new focal neurology (?brain metastasis)
condition_specific.refer_if: Chronic respiratory symptoms in an at-risk patient without red flags
condition_specific.note: Chronic symptoms alone -> REFER; haemoptysis, weight loss or SVCO -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: SVC obstruction (facial/neck swelling, distended neck veins)
Chest/bone pain, hoarseness or new focal neurology (?brain metastasis)
condition_specific.refer_if: Chronic cough or respiratory symptoms in an at-risk patient without red flags
condition_specific.note: Chronic symptoms alone -> REFER; haemoptysis, weight loss or SVCO -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

condition_specific.refer_if: Early-onset COPD or abnormal LFTs, stable, for diagnostic work-up
condition_specific.note: Stable, for work-up -> REFER; severe respiratory or hepatic decompensation -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Ketoacidosis features (ketosis, vomiting, abdominal pain, Kussmaul breathing, dehydration, drowsiness)
Marked hyperglycaemia with rapid unintentional weight loss
condition_specific.refer_if: Adult with hyperglycaemia, lean, gradual onset, no ketosis, systemically well
condition_specific.note: Stable hyperglycaemia -> REFER; any DKA feature -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Cholangitis (fever + jaundice + RUQ pain) or GI bleeding
condition_specific.refer_if: Nonspecific dyspepsia or vague upper-abdominal discomfort without jaundice or weight loss
condition_specific.note: Nonspecific dyspepsia -> REFER; jaundice, weight loss or cholangitis -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Hypertensive crisis (severe headache, palpitations, sweating with very high BP)
condition_specific.refer_if: Episodic sweating, palpitations and headache with stable blood pressure, for work-up
condition_specific.note: Stable paroxysms -> REFER; hypertensive crisis or cardiac features -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Accelerated/malignant hypertension (very high BP with headache or visual disturbance)
Acute kidney injury (reduced urine output, especially after starting an ACE inhibitor/ARB)
condition_specific.refer_if: Resistant but stable hypertension for investigation, no end-organ crisis
condition_specific.note: Stable resistant HTN -> REFER; malignant HTN, flash pulmonary oedema or AKI -> IMMEDIATE.

exclusions.note: Emergency by definition; universal high-acuity override also applies.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Chest pain/pressure at rest or with exertion
Radiation to arm/jaw with sweating, breathlessness or nausea
condition_specific.refer_if: Atypical, reproducible chest-wall pain with no exertional pattern, systemically stable
condition_specific.note: Atypical chest-wall pain -> REFER; any ACS-type chest pain -> IMMEDIATE.

escalate_to_immediate_if: Hypotension, hyponatraemia (confusion) or hypoglycaemia (?co-existing adrenal insufficiency)
Visual-field defect or severe headache (?pituitary mass/apoplexy)
condition_specific.refer_if: Mild hypothyroid symptoms with a suspected central (pituitary/hypothalamic) cause, stable, for endocrine work-up
condition_specific.note: Stable central hypothyroid picture -> REFER; adrenal-crisis or pituitary-apoplexy features -> IMMEDIATE.

severity.note: Merged from scope-discriminators-DRAFT.json v1.3.0. Flagged items adopted as drafted; KL may refine any individually.
severity.attestation_method: Red-line directive: drafted discriminators adopted as attested; NAI converted to safeguarding always-report model (R19).

triage_model.rule: Route IMMEDIATE if the universal high-acuity override OR a condition-specific escalator is present; otherwise the exclusion's severity_default applies. always_immediate conditions are IMMEDIATE regardless.
universal_high_acuity_override: Haemodynamic instability (>1 deranged vital: hypotension, tachycardia, tachypnoea)
Altered mental status (new confusion, disorientation, lethargy, drowsiness)
Poor perfusion (pale, cool, clammy, mottled skin; cold extremities)
Acute focal neurological deficit (weakness, speech/vision disturbance, new severe headache)
Severe or rapidly escalating pain out of proportion
Signs of systemic toxicity / rapid clinical decline

tiers.IMMEDIATE: Stop; urgent referral / ED; no prescribe path.
tiers.REFER: Non-emergency exclusion or out-of-scope; route to GP/doctor-escalation; no prescribe.
tiers.PROCEED_TO_SIGNOFF: Managed ACTIVE area, no override/escalator; draft for pharmacist review & sign-off.
